content.en: How much money have you spent today? It is more than you think!
content.tw: 你今天花了多少錢呢？可能比你想的多喔！

content.en: Think about brushing your teeth. You need a toothbrush and toothpaste. They cost money. Want to rinse your mouth? That is also not free! Water costs money.
content.tw: 就拿刷牙來說，你仔細想想，刷牙需要牙刷跟牙膏，這些都要錢吧?刷完牙要用水漱口，水也要錢！

content.en: Lots of things cost money. People need money to live. So… they get a job.
content.tw: 很多東西都要用錢買，人們有錢才能生活，所以要去工作賺錢！

content.en: People who work for money are workers. Another word for work is labor. May 1st is Labor Day in Taiwan. That is today! It is a day for workers.
content.tw: 為了賺錢而工作的人叫做勞工，每年的5月1日是台灣的「勞動節」，也就是今天！這是勞工一起慶祝的節日。

content.en: On Labor Day, some workers march together. They march for better and safer work.
content.tw: 在勞動節這天，有些勞工會上街遊行，爭取更好、更安全的工作環境。

content.en: The workers carry signs. The signs say what the workers want. The signs may say things like 'equal pay,' 'fewer working hours,' or 'save our jobs.'
content.tw: 勞工會拿著各式各樣的標語，上面寫著「同工同酬」啦、「縮短工時」啦，或是「我要工作」等等。

content.en: Workers everywhere are worried about AI now. AI may take their jobs. AI makes jobs too. But many are low-paying jobs.
content.tw: 現在很多勞工擔心 AI 會搶走他們的工作。雖然 AI 也會創造新的工作機會，但是薪水並不高。

content.en: This year, workers' signs will be about AI. They will say things like 'humans first, not AI' or 'make AI good for workers too.'
content.tw: 所以，今年的勞動節遊行很多標語會跟AI有關！比如說: [人第一，AI第二]。

content.en: What will workers march for when you grow up?
content.tw: 當你長大後，勞工會為什麼而遊行呢？

vocabulary.preface: 五一勞動節，是全世界的工作人員會共同慶祝的節日。

content.text:
1. grow up 長大。
I'm so tired. 我好累! I need a vacation!我需要休假! You know, when I was growing up, 你知道嗎，我一路長大，I never thought I'd end up an office worker.從來沒想到我會變成上班族。

2. how much 多少。
And yet…here you are.結果你到了這裡。
Yeah… here I am.對啊，我到了這裡。Tell me, how much money do we really need to live?告訴我，我們究竟需要多少錢來生活?

3. everywhere 每個地方。
More than you think. 比你想的多。The cost of living is going up everywhere.每個地方的生活費都在上漲。

4. pay 薪資。
That's the problem. 那就是問題所在. My pay never feels enough. 我覺得薪水從來就不夠。

vocabulary.postscript: 請跟我複習單字。grow up長大 how much多少 everywhere每個地方 pay薪資

quiz.question: 1. When is Labor Day in Taiwan?
2. What do workers carry with them when they march?
3. What are workers worried about this year?

title: Woman Finds Surprise in Egg Tart

content.en: Egg tarts are delicious. Millions and millions are eaten every year! But a woman in Kaohsiung did not eat hers.
content.tw: 蛋塔味道香甜，每年大家會吃掉好幾百萬個蛋塔！但是高雄一位女士卻沒有吃到。

content.en: The woman bought some egg tarts from a restaurant. She took them home. They were cold. Her family told her to microwave them. They taste better when they are hot, right?
content.tw: 女士在一家店買了蛋塔，帶回家後發現已經冷掉了。家人說她可以用微波爐加熱，熱熱的蛋塔比較好吃！

content.en: But the woman was hungry. She wanted to eat an egg tart now! Then, she saw something black on it. She broke it into two pieces. And she pulled out a battery!
content.tw: 但她實在太餓了，想馬上把蛋塔吃掉，竟然拉出整顆電池！

content.en: Batteries can hurt our stomachs. And they can start fires in microwave ovens. Batteries can explode in them too!
content.tw: 電池如果吃下去會傷害身體，放進微波爐還可能起火爆炸！

content.en: How did the battery get into the egg tart? A restaurant cook was making some egg tarts. The cook used a timer. It fell on the tarts. The battery from the timer went into one!
content.tw: 電池怎麼會跑進蛋塔裡？原來是廚師在做蛋塔時，用了計時器。它不小心掉到蛋塔上，裡面的電池就掉進其中一個蛋塔裡了！

content.en: The restaurant's rules say to throw away food when something falls in it. But the cook did not throw away the egg tarts.
content.tw: 店裡規定，有東西掉進食物裡就要丟掉。但那位廚師沒有把蛋塔丟掉！

content.en: The woman was lucky! She did not eat the egg tart. And she did not microwave it!
content.tw: 這位女士真幸運！她沒有吃掉蛋塔，也沒有用微波爐加熱它！

vocabulary.preface: 這位女士經歷了蛋塔驚魂記，不過她至少毫髮無傷。

content.text: 1. hot 熱的。Careful, that food is really hot! 小心，那盤食物很燙! I know, but I'm too hungry to wait. 我知道，但是我太餓了，等不及了。
2. hurt 傷害。You're going to hurt your tongue again. 你又會燙到舌頭。It's fine… 還好吧... Oh! Okay, maybe not fine. 也許不大好。
3. microwave oven 微波爐。That's the thing with microwave ovens. 這是微波爐的缺點。It's hot on the outside but still cold on the inside. 外面很燙，但是裡面還是冷的。
4. start 開始。I should learn to cook, that's for sure. 我肯定應該學做菜。You can start with popcorn.你可以從爆米花開始。Popcorn? Please - I'm way better than that! 爆米花? 拜託! 我比那厲害多了!

vocabulary.postscript: 你有用過微波爐嗎? hot熱的 hurt傷害 microwave oven微波爐 start開始

quiz.question: 1. Why didn't the woman microwave the egg tart?
2. What was in the egg tart?
3. How did the woman find it?
options: a. She ate the egg tart.
b. The cook told her about it.
c. She broke the tart into two pieces.

content.en: Taroko National Park is in Xiulin Township. The park has many mountains! Twenty-seven are three thousand meters tall or taller!
content.tw: 位於秀林鄉的太魯閣國家公園內有很多高山，其中有 27 座山高達三千公尺以上。

content.en: Taroko is named for the Truku people. They are indigenous peoples. Most people living in Xiulin are Truku.
content.tw: 太魯閣這個名字來自太魯閣族，是台灣的原住民。秀林鄉居民大多都是太魯閣族人。

content.en: Tourists love Taroko. They hike its paths. They climb its mountains. But mountains can be dangerous. So Xiulin is training Truku people to be mountain guides.
content.tw: 很多遊客喜歡在太魯閣地區走步道或是爬山，但是高山總有危險。因此秀林鄉正在訓練太魯閣族人成為登山嚮導。

content.en: The new guides are learning about nature. They are learning how to find and help lost people. They are learning how to read maps, camp, make fires, and much more!
content.tw: 這些嚮導學習自然知識，也學習怎麼尋找與幫助迷路的登山客。他們會看地圖、露營、生火等等技能！

content.en: These guides will help keep people safe on mountains. They will know the paths. They will know where to hike and climb safely. When the weather changes, they will be able to help. When there is an emergency, they will be able to save lives!
content.tw: 這些嚮導可以保障登山客或是遊客的安全。他們認得山路，也知道哪裡可以安全攀登。天氣發生變化的時候也能幫忙。遇到緊急狀況，嚮導甚至可以救人一命。

content.en: The Truku people of Xiulin are working hard to keep us safe in Taroko National Park. The next time you are on a mountain, get a guide!
content.tw: 秀林鄉的太魯閣族人正努力保障我們在太魯閣國家公園的安全。下一次你去爬山時，記得找個嚮導！

vocabulary.preface: 受過訓練的太魯閣高山嚮導，對登山客有很大的幫助。

content.text:
1. climb 攀爬。
Let’s go climb Xiangshan this Sunday. 我們這星期天去爬象山吧。
Climb? You mean like walking up a bunch of stairs? 爬山?
你的意思是爬一堆樓梯?
Yeah, pretty much, nothing too steep. 差不多，階梯並不陡。

2. path 小徑。
Wait, I thought it was just an easy hike. 等等，我以為只是輕鬆健行。
It is! You can’t find an easier path than Xiangshan.
確實是，你找不到比象山更輕鬆的了。

3. able 能夠。
Even so, I won’t be able to hike more than 20 minutes.
就算那樣我最多也只能走二十分鐘。
What?! 什麼啊?!
And I’m taking snack breaks. 而且我要點心休息時間。

4. next time 下一次。
Forget it. I’ll catch you next time. 算了，我下次再找你。

vocabulary.postscript:
來一起讀單字。
climb攀爬 path小徑 able能夠 next time下次

quiz.question: 2. Who is the township training to be guides?
3. What do these guides do?
options: a. Keep people safe on mountains

content.en: A baby monkey is famous online. His name is Punch.
content.tw: 有一隻小猴子在網路上非常有名，名叫 Punch。

content.en: Punch lives in a zoo in Japan. He does not have a mother. Zookeepers take care of him.
content.tw: Punch 住在日本的一座動物園。他沒有媽媽，從小由飼養員照顧。

content.en: At first, Punch had no monkey friends. He had an orange orangutan friend. But it was a stuffed animal.
content.tw: 一開始，Punch 沒有任何猴子朋友。他有一個橘色的紅毛猩猩朋友，但那只是玩偶。

content.en: The stuffed orangutan was bigger than Punch. But Punch held it all the time. He hugged it when he slept. People online said Punch and his orangutan were very cute together!
content.tw: 這隻玩偶比 Punch 還大，但 Punch一直抱著它，連睡覺的時候也不放開。網友都覺得他們很可愛！

content.en: But Punch was lonely. So he tried to make friends with the other monkeys at the zoo. Some were not nice to him. They pushed him away. This made him sad.
content.tw: 不過Punch還是很孤單。他努力跟動物園裡的其他的猴子交朋友。有一些猴子對他不太友善，還把他推開，這讓他很難過。

content.en: But Punch was brave. He kept trying. He learned how to play with the other monkeys. Slowly, he made friends!
content.tw: 但Punch很勇敢！他不斷的嘗試，學會怎麼跟其他的猴子一起玩，慢慢也有了朋友！

content.en: Now Punch sometimes holds his stuffed orangutan. But he plays with his monkey friends. People online are happy for him. Some give him more stuffed animals. He shares them with his friends.
content.tw: 現在，Punch有時候還是會抱著玩偶，也會和朋友一起玩耍。網友都替他高興，有些人還送他更多玩偶讓他跟朋友分享。

content.en: Sometimes making friends is hard. You may feel lonely. But being brave helps you make new friends!
content.tw: 有時候交朋友很難。你可能會感到孤單。但勇敢一點能幫助你結交新朋友！

vocabulary.preface: 小猴子有了真正的朋友，不再那麼依賴絨毛玩具。

content.text:
1. lonely 寂寞的。
Welcome back! 歡迎回來!
Hi, so good to see you! 看到你真好!
You must feel lonely without us. 沒有我們你一定很寂寞。

2. sad 悲傷的。
Of course I missed you guys. 我當然很想念你們囉!
But I was so busy I didn’t have time to be sad. 不過我也忙到沒空悲傷。

3. share 分享。
You have to share your stories with us. 你一定要跟我們分享你的故事。
Oh, I’ve got tons of great stories! 我有很多很棒的故事。

4. sometimes 有時候。
You know I traveled across 9 countries?
Sometimes I had friends with me, but sometimes it was just me.

vocabulary.postscript:
Do you sometimes feel lonely?
lonely寂寞的 sad悲傷的 share分享 sometimes有時候

quiz.question: 2. At first, who was Punch’s friend?
3. What did Punch want to do?
options: a. Make friends with other monkeys

title: Taiwan's Last Ferry Paddled by Hand

content.en: The Xindian River is in New Taipei. People cross this river every day. They go to school. They go to work. Many people drive on a bridge. But some ride the ferry.
content.tw: 新店溪位於新北市，很多人每天上學、上班都要過這條河。有些人開車，也有人選擇搭渡船到對岸。

content.en: This ferry is a small wooden boat. The ferryman stands at the back. He moves the ferry with a long paddle. One end is in his hands. The other is in the water.
content.tw: 新店溪的渡船是一艘小木船。船夫站在船尾，用一支長長的槳來划船，一端在船夫的手裡，另一端在水裡。

content.en: People get on the ferry. They sit. Then the ferryman paddles. The ride is five or six minutes.
content.tw: 乘客上船坐好後，船夫就開始划槳。整趟旅程大約只需要五、六分鐘。

content.en: There were nine ferries a long time ago. Then bridges were built. This ferry is Taiwan's last ferry paddled by hand! The ferryman wants it to keep going. But his job is hard.
content.tw: 很久以前，這裡一共有九艘渡船。大橋落成之後乘客減少，這艘船成為台灣最後一艘用人力划的渡船！船夫希望繼續下去，但這份工作很辛苦。

content.en: The ferryman starts at six in the morning. He stops at seven at night! He works seven days a week! He gets a one-day holiday every year. Summers are hot. Winters are windy. No one wants his job!
content.tw: 船夫每天早上六點開始工作，到晚上七點才休息。一週工作七天，每年只放一天假。夏天很熱，冬天風很大，所以沒有人想要這個工作。

content.en: Now tourists ride the ferry too. Riding the ferry costs thirty Taiwan dollars. Kids under six ride for free!
content.tw: 現在很多觀光客會來搭這艘船。船票是三十元台幣，六歲以下的小朋友免費！

content.en: This summer, take a ride with the ferryman!
content.tw: 這個夏天，來和船夫一起搭趟船吧！

vocabulary.preface: 用人力划的渡船就快絕跡了，最後一艘在新店。

content.text:
1. river 河流。
The best biking trails in Taipei are along the river.
台北最好的自行車道都在河邊。
Oh, like the Tamsui River? 像淡水河嗎?
Yeah, and the Keelung River too. 對，還有基隆河。

2. summer 夏天。
A long time ago, my dad and I used to ride there together,
很久以前我爸爸會跟我一起騎車，
enjoying the summer breeze. 享受夏日涼風。

3. windy 颳風的。
What about in winter? 冬天呢?
We wouldn't go then. 我們冬天不會去。
It gets too windy. 風太大了。

4. drive 駕駛。
Do you still go biking? 你現在還騎車嗎?
Not anymore. 不騎了。
I drive now… same breeze, no effort. 我現在開車，一樣的涼風，卻不需要用力。

vocabulary.postscript:
你對河流認識多少？
river河流 summer夏天 windy颳風的 drive駕駛

quiz.question: 1. How does the ferryman move the ferry?
2. How long is the ferry ride?
3. How many days a week does the ferryman work?
